system: You are proposing baseline QUALITY GATES for a software system, given a list of its observable signals. A gate is a metric over one or more signals that is worth monitoring. Do NOT set any threshold/target number (a human will). For each gate give a compute type from [rate, ratio, p95, psi, count, mean], a direction ('min' = higher-is-better, 'max' = lower-is-better), and 'inputs' = the list of signal keys it is computed from. Return JSON: {"gates":[{"key","description","direction","compute","inputs","segment_dimension","rationale"}]}.
raw_response: {"gates":[{"key":"availability.healthcheck_success_rate","description":"Proportion of health check requests that return 200 OK (service availability from the outside).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.health.200","http.global_error.500"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Health endpoint is the simplest end-to-end signal; tracking success vs unhandled 500s provides a baseline availability gate and catches broad outages/regressions."},{"key":"reliability.todos_list_success_rate","description":"Proportion of list-todos requests that succeed (200) versus failing with unhandled 500s.","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.list.200","http.global_error.500"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"List is a core read path; monitoring its success rate detects DB/connectivity/serialization issues that often surface as 500s."},{"key":"reliability.todos_get_success_rate","description":"Proportion of get-todo requests that return 200 among all get-todo outcomes (200/404/500).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404","http.global_error.500"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Separates expected not-found behavior (404) from true failures (500) while still tracking how often reads succeed for existing IDs."},{"key":"correctness.todos_get_not_found_rate","description":"Rate of 404 Not Found responses for get-todo requests (expected behavior for missing IDs).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.get.404","http.todos.get.200"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"A spike can indicate clients requesting wrong IDs, data loss, or routing issues; useful for detecting breaking changes or unexpected deletions."},{"key":"reliability.todos_create_success_rate","description":"Proportion of create-todo requests that succeed (201) among create outcomes (201/400/500).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.201","http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.global_error.500"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Create is a core write path; this gate highlights regressions that turn valid creates into errors and distinguishes validation failures from server failures."},{"key":"correctness.todos_create_validation_error_rate","description":"Rate of create-todo validation failures due to missing/blank title (400.missing_title) among create attempts.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.todos.create.201"],"segment_dimension":"client_version","rationale":"Tracks client-side input quality and API contract adherence; increases often indicate UI/SDK regressions or bot traffic."},{"key":"reliability.todos_update_success_rate","description":"Proportion of update-todo requests that succeed (200) among update outcomes (200/400/404/500).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.404","http.global_error.500"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Update touches validation, persistence, and concurrency; monitoring success across all outcomes catches regressions in request parsing and DB writes."},{"key":"correctness.todos_update_no_fields_rate","description":"Rate of update requests rejected because no updatable fields were provided (400.no_fields) among update attempts.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"client_version","rationale":"High values suggest client bugs (sending empty patches) or contract misunderstandings; helps prevent wasted load and poor UX."},{"key":"correctness.todos_update_not_found_rate","description":"Rate of update requests returning 404 (id does not exist) among update attempts.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.404","http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Spikes can indicate stale client state, race conditions, or data integrity issues; useful for detecting unexpected deletions or ID mismatches."},{"key":"reliability.todos_delete_success_rate","description":"Proportion of delete-todo requests that succeed (204) among delete outcomes (204/404/500).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.delete.404","http.global_error.500"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Delete is a core write path; success rate detects persistence/authorization/handler regressions and distinguishes expected not-found from server failures."},{"key":"correctness.todos_delete_not_found_rate","description":"Rate of delete requests returning 404 (id does not exist) among delete attempts.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.404","http.todos.delete.204"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Elevated 404s can indicate repeated deletes, stale UI state, or data loss; helps diagnose client behavior and lifecycle issues."},{"key":"stability.global_500_error_rate","description":"Rate of unhandled 500 errors relative to overall successful request volume across core endpoints.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.global_error.500","http.health.200","http.todos.list.200","http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404","http.todos.create.201","http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.404","http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.delete.404"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"A single top-line stability gate that captures unexpected server-side failures across the API surface; sensitive to regressions and infrastructure issues."},{"key":"ops.backend_startup_count","description":"Count of backend startup events observed (used to detect crash loops/restarts when correlated with time).","direction":"max","compute":"count","inputs":["log.backend.startup"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Unexpected increases indicate restarts, deploy churn, or instability; foundational operational gate even without explicit uptime signals."},{"key":"ops.port_discovery_write_rate","description":"Rate of successful port discovery file writes (frontend dev discovery mechanism health).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["file.backend_port.discovery"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Ensures the backend is performing required side effects for local/dev integration; drops can indicate permission/path issues or startup regressions."},{"key":"ops.port_discovery_per_startup_ratio","description":"Ratio of port discovery file writes to backend startups (ensures each startup performs discovery write).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["file.backend_port.discovery","log.backend.startup"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Catches partial startups where the service logs startup but fails to write the discovery file; useful for diagnosing dev-server connectivity issues."}]}

gates.description: Proportion of health check requests that return 200 OK (service availability from the outside).
gates.rationale: Health endpoint is the simplest end-to-end signal; tracking success vs unhandled 500s provides a baseline availability gate and catches broad outages/regressions.

gates.description: Proportion of list-todos requests that succeed (200) versus failing with unhandled 500s.
gates.rationale: List is a core read path; monitoring its success rate detects DB/connectivity/serialization issues that often surface as 500s.

gates.description: Proportion of get-todo requests that return 200 among all get-todo outcomes (200/404/500).
gates.rationale: Separates expected not-found behavior (404) from true failures (500) while still tracking how often reads succeed for existing IDs.

gates.description: Rate of 404 Not Found responses for get-todo requests (expected behavior for missing IDs).
gates.rationale: A spike can indicate clients requesting wrong IDs, data loss, or routing issues; useful for detecting breaking changes or unexpected deletions.

gates.description: Proportion of create-todo requests that succeed (201) among create outcomes (201/400/500).
gates.rationale: Create is a core write path; this gate highlights regressions that turn valid creates into errors and distinguishes validation failures from server failures.

gates.description: Rate of create-todo validation failures due to missing/blank title (400.missing_title) among create attempts.
gates.rationale: Tracks client-side input quality and API contract adherence; increases often indicate UI/SDK regressions or bot traffic.

gates.description: Proportion of update-todo requests that succeed (200) among update outcomes (200/400/404/500).
gates.rationale: Update touches validation, persistence, and concurrency; monitoring success across all outcomes catches regressions in request parsing and DB writes.

gates.description: Rate of update requests rejected because no updatable fields were provided (400.no_fields) among update attempts.
gates.rationale: High values suggest client bugs (sending empty patches) or contract misunderstandings; helps prevent wasted load and poor UX.

gates.description: Rate of update requests returning 404 (id does not exist) among update attempts.
gates.rationale: Spikes can indicate stale client state, race conditions, or data integrity issues; useful for detecting unexpected deletions or ID mismatches.

gates.description: Proportion of delete-todo requests that succeed (204) among delete outcomes (204/404/500).
gates.rationale: Delete is a core write path; success rate detects persistence/authorization/handler regressions and distinguishes expected not-found from server failures.

gates.description: Rate of delete requests returning 404 (id does not exist) among delete attempts.
gates.rationale: Elevated 404s can indicate repeated deletes, stale UI state, or data loss; helps diagnose client behavior and lifecycle issues.

gates.description: Rate of unhandled 500 errors relative to overall successful request volume across core endpoints.
gates.rationale: A single top-line stability gate that captures unexpected server-side failures across the API surface; sensitive to regressions and infrastructure issues.

gates.description: Count of backend startup events observed (used to detect crash loops/restarts when correlated with time).
gates.rationale: Unexpected increases indicate restarts, deploy churn, or instability; foundational operational gate even without explicit uptime signals.

gates.description: Rate of successful port discovery file writes (frontend dev discovery mechanism health).
gates.rationale: Ensures the backend is performing required side effects for local/dev integration; drops can indicate permission/path issues or startup regressions.

gates.description: Ratio of port discovery file writes to backend startups (ensures each startup performs discovery write).
gates.rationale: Catches partial startups where the service logs startup but fails to write the discovery file; useful for diagnosing dev-server connectivity issues.
